segments: Ancillary Operations (Tenant Reinsurance, Merchandise Sales, Third-Party Management)

supply_chain.dependencies: Self-storage operations depend on construction materials and labor for development and redevelopment projects
supply_chain.risks: Cost and availability of construction materials and labor; supply chain disruptions affecting development projects; international trade disputes and tariffs impacting costs for steel, lumber and other materials

regulatory_risks: Emergency regulations limiting rents or restricting access to facilities in response to wildfires, floods, or public health crises (e.g., California wildfire regulations, COVID-19 restrictions)
Property tax increases due to changes in assessed values or elimination of Proposition 13 benefits in California
Wage regulations including minimum wage increases
REIT qualification requirements under Internal Revenue Code - failure to qualify would result in federal corporate income tax
State and local business licensing laws and regulations
Zoning and permitting limitations for development projects
Consumer protection laws related to lien sales
Privacy laws including California Privacy Rights Act and California Consumer Privacy Act
Americans with Disabilities Act compliance requirements
Insurance licensing and regulation by state departments of insurance
Securities laws and SEC reporting requirements
Potential changes to federal tax laws affecting REITs or shareholders
California SB 253 and SB 261 requiring climate-related disclosures starting 2026
Rent control or eviction restrictions imposed by local governments

sanction_risks: International trade disputes including U.S. trade tariffs and retaliatory tariffs could result in inflationary pressures impacting costs for steel, lumber and other materials for development projects
